author: Kuioma
description: Rename pasted images with customizable templates and frontmatter-driven naming - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Kuioma
manifest.description: Rename pasted images with customizable templates and frontmatter-driven naming
manifest.name: Image Renamer

name: Image Renamer

releases.author: Kuioma
releases.description:
Features

-     Instant paste rename — intercepts paste events and writes images with the template name directly, so the old filename never appears in your note

-     Drag-drop rename — auto-rename images dragged from Finder into a note

-     Configurable templates — {{date}}, {{time}}, {{datetime}}, {{counter}}, {{original}}, {{filename}}, and custom frontmatter variables (with {{key:default}} fallback syntax)

-     Auto counter per note — sequential numbering via image-counter frontmatter, auto-decrements when images are deleted

-     Per-note image folder — override the default folder via image-folder in frontmatter

-     Batch organize — rename all images in the current note or across the entire vault

-     Unreferenced images finder — locate and clean up orphaned images (move to trash or permanently delete)

-     Bilingual UI — English and Chinese, switchable in settings

    Installation

    Available in Obsidian Community Plugins (pending review). For manual installation:

    1. Download main.js and manifest.json from this release
    2. Create .obsidian/plugins/image-renamer/ in your vault
    3. Copy both files into the folder
    4. Enable in Settings → Community Plugins
